title: NBC News correspondent receives 'all clear' while reporting from Israeli bomb shelter
author: NBC News
fullText: We heard you just had to get to safety and to get inside. Uh what can you tell us that you're hearing where you are? What's happening? >> Yeah, it was kind of interesting because while we've been seeing these hearing these all day long for the last several days, all night long, uh you know, this was a little bit different because almost every time, actually, every single time this latest round of fighting, we've been getting alerts on our phones, maybe something between 5 to 10 minutes before the actual siren goes off. Uh oh, Matt, I think we lost maybe your microphone. It looks like you might be plugging it in. Uh Matt and his crew just had to get to safety as you just heard him mention there a moment ago because they were hearing sounds in the ether. Hold on. Wait a minute. I think we might have you back. Matt, can you hear us? Okay. >> Yes. Sorry. I'll tell you what happened is that I'm actually speaking to you on a cell phone, of course, here on Israel's network. And what we got was an allclear. So that's why my phone cut out because the allcle notice was actually cutting in. Um that tends to happen. So yeah, we can leave this room. Um but we don't understand why there wasn't a warning sign which was a very interesting development. We understand from very early reports and we don't know this. We haven't verified this that this might have been a missile launch from Lebanon which is one of the reasons why we don't have that expansive long warning time ahead of time. We thank you for watching and remember, stay updated on breaking news and top stories on the NBC News app or watch live on our YouTube channel.
